name: OpenCost Kubernetes Cost Monitoring API
what_it_does: Open-source Kubernetes cost monitoring tool with REST API — enabling platform teams to measure and allocate real-time and historical Kubernetes costs per namespace, deployment, pod, label, and team, with CNCF incubating project status, AWS/GCP/Azure cloud provider integration, and self-hosted deployment alongside Prometheus.
use_cases: Querying Kubernetes cost allocation via OpenCost API for team chargeback and showback
Monitoring pod and namespace costs via OpenCost API for real-time FinOps visibility
Exporting cost data via OpenCost API for custom FinOps dashboards and reporting
Integrating cost data via OpenCost API with Grafana for cost observability alongside metrics
Benchmarking workload efficiency via OpenCost API for cost optimization identification
not_for: Multi-cloud cost management beyond Kubernetes (use CloudHealth or Apptio for broader FinOps)
Managed SaaS without self-hosting (OpenCost requires deployment in your cluster)
best_when: A platform engineering team wants free, open-source Kubernetes cost visibility deployed in their own cluster, with Prometheus integration and CNCF governance.
avoid_when: You need managed SaaS, multi-cloud cost management beyond K8s, or don't run Kubernetes workloads.

auth.notes: No authentication — OpenCost API is unauthenticated by default (internal cluster service). Deployed as a Kubernetes service: access via kubectl port-forward or internal cluster networking. REST API at http://opencost.{namespace}:9003/ within cluster. Production security: use network policies or Ingress authentication to protect the API. No external API keys — security is at network layer. Prometheus metrics also exposed at :9090.

pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tiers; Kubecost (commercial fork) offers enterprise features
pricing.notes: CNCF Incubating project (since 2022). Originally created by Stackwatch (now Kubecost). Apache 2.0 license. Kubecost is the commercial version with enterprise features (multi-cluster, SSO, etc.). OpenCost: single-cluster cost monitoring. Cloud pricing: integrates with AWS Cost and Usage Report, GCP Billing, Azure Billing for actual node costs. Prometheus: relies on Prometheus for metrics collection. Resource pricing: configurable custom prices or cloud API pricing. Cost allocation: namespace, pod, container, label dimensions. UI: optional Kubecost community UI. Data storage: local (15 days default); Thanos/Cortex for long-term.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: NO AUTH by default — secure with network policies/Ingress auth. In-cluster service. Prometheus dependency. 15-day default retention. CNCF Incubating. Apache 2.0. Kubecost is commercial fork with enterprise features.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All GET requests are idempotent — historical cost data is deterministic. Cost allocation queries by date range are idempotent. No write operations.
known_agent_gotchas: NO AUTHENTICATION by default — add network policy or Ingress auth layer before exposing outside cluster
In-cluster access only: use kubectl port-forward (port 9003) for local development access
Prometheus required: OpenCost reads K8s metrics from Prometheus — deploy Prometheus first
Cloud pricing accuracy: configure AWS/GCP/Azure billing integration for actual costs vs estimated
15-day default data retention: configure Thanos or remote write for longer cost history
Single-cluster only: use Kubecost Enterprise for multi-cluster cost aggregation

security_score_components.security_notes: CRITICAL: No authentication by default — exposed to anyone with cluster network access. Add network policies restricting API access to authorized pods only. Prometheus dependency: secure Prometheus separately. Open-source: code is auditable. CNCF governance: security response process via CNCF SSCP. Self-hosted: data never leaves your cluster (privacy advantage). Cloud billing integration: requires cloud IAM role for cost data access. In-cluster HTTP (not HTTPS) by default — use Ingress with TLS for external exposure.
